item.title: The Documentary Got It Wrong: Brooke Preston
item.description:
The murder of 21 year old Brooke Preston gained renewed attention after the Hulu documentary Dead Asleep explored whether her killer, Randy Herman Jr., was sleepwalking during the attack. The film sparked major backlash from Brooke’s family, who argued the documentary focused too heavily on Herman’s defense while minimizing Brooke’s story and the impact on her loved ones.
In 2017, Herman stabbed Brooke to death inside their Florida home before calling 911 and later claiming he had no memory of the attack. His legal team argued he committed the killing during a sleepwalking episode, but prosecutors rejected that explanation during trial proceedings.
Brooke’s family publicly criticized the documentary after its release, saying it unfairly framed the case around Herman’s perspective and caused additional pain for those closest to Brooke. Randy Herman Jr. was convicted of first degree murder and sentenced to life in prison

media:content.media:title: The Documentary Got It Wrong: Brooke Preston
media:content.media:description:
The murder of 21 year old Brooke Preston gained renewed attention after the Hulu documentary Dead Asleep explored whether her killer, Randy Herman Jr., was sleepwalking during the attack. The film sparked major backlash from Brooke’s family, who argued the documentary focused too heavily on Herman’s defense while minimizing Brooke’s story and the impact on her loved ones.
In 2017, Herman stabbed Brooke to death inside their Florida home before calling 911 and later claiming he had no memory of the attack. His legal team argued he committed the killing during a sleepwalking episode, but prosecutors rejected that explanation during trial proceedings.
Brooke’s family publicly criticized the documentary after its release, saying it unfairly framed the case around Herman’s perspective and caused additional pain for those closest to Brooke. Randy Herman Jr. was convicted of first degree murder and sentenced to life in prison

item.title: The Very Suspicious Case Of Grant Solomon
item.description:
In 2020, 18 year old Grant Solomon died in Tennessee after being run over by a truck driven by his father outside a baseball training facility. Authorities ruled the death an accident, but the circumstances surrounding the incident quickly sparked public suspicion and controversy.
Grant’s mother, Angie Solomon, publicly questioned the official findings and alleged there had been ongoing family court disputes and concerns within the household before Grant’s death. Online discussion surrounding the case intensified as people examined inconsistencies, legal records, and interviews connected to the investigation.
The case gained widespread attention on social media and true crime platforms because many believe unanswered questions still remain. Despite ongoing public scrutiny and calls for further investigation, Grant’s death officially remains classified as an accident.

media:content.media:title: The Very Suspicious Case Of Grant Solomon
media:content.media:description:
In 2020, 18 year old Grant Solomon died in Tennessee after being run over by a truck driven by his father outside a baseball training facility. Authorities ruled the death an accident, but the circumstances surrounding the incident quickly sparked public suspicion and controversy.
Grant’s mother, Angie Solomon, publicly questioned the official findings and alleged there had been ongoing family court disputes and concerns within the household before Grant’s death. Online discussion surrounding the case intensified as people examined inconsistencies, legal records, and interviews connected to the investigation.
The case gained widespread attention on social media and true crime platforms because many believe unanswered questions still remain. Despite ongoing public scrutiny and calls for further investigation, Grant’s death officially remains classified as an accident.

item.title: Diane Downs Showed No Empathy After Her Children Were Shot
item.description:
In 1983, Oregon mother Diane Downs claimed a stranger shot her and her three children during a roadside attack. Investigators quickly became suspicious after noticing inconsistencies in her story and what many described as her unusually detached behavior following the shooting.
Authorities later concluded Downs herself was responsible for the attack that killed her daughter Cheryl and seriously injured her other two children. Prosecutors argued she viewed her children as obstacles to a relationship with a man who reportedly did not want children.
The case became infamous because of Downs’ interviews, courtroom behavior, and apparent lack of remorse throughout the investigation and trial. She was convicted in 1984 and sentenced to life in prison, where she remains today.

media:content.media:title: Diane Downs Showed No Empathy After Her Children Were Shot
media:content.media:description:
In 1983, Oregon mother Diane Downs claimed a stranger shot her and her three children during a roadside attack. Investigators quickly became suspicious after noticing inconsistencies in her story and what many described as her unusually detached behavior following the shooting.
Authorities later concluded Downs herself was responsible for the attack that killed her daughter Cheryl and seriously injured her other two children. Prosecutors argued she viewed her children as obstacles to a relationship with a man who reportedly did not want children.
The case became infamous because of Downs’ interviews, courtroom behavior, and apparent lack of remorse throughout the investigation and trial. She was convicted in 1984 and sentenced to life in prison, where she remains today.

item.title: The Kaylee Sawyer Case: Who's Responsible?
item.description:
In July 2016, 23 year old Kaylee Sawyer disappeared after leaving a party in Bend, Oregon. Hours later, investigators discovered her body in a remote area, launching a homicide investigation that shocked the local community.
Authorities later arrested Edwin Lara, a campus security officer, after evidence linked him to the case. Prosecutors said Lara used his position and vehicle to gain Kaylee’s trust before abducting and killing her.
Lara later pleaded guilty and received life in prison. The case sparked widespread discussion about hiring practices, background checks, and safety concerns involving private security personnel.

media:content.media:title: The Kaylee Sawyer Case: Who's Responsible?
media:content.media:description:
In July 2016, 23 year old Kaylee Sawyer disappeared after leaving a party in Bend, Oregon. Hours later, investigators discovered her body in a remote area, launching a homicide investigation that shocked the local community.
Authorities later arrested Edwin Lara, a campus security officer, after evidence linked him to the case. Prosecutors said Lara used his position and vehicle to gain Kaylee’s trust before abducting and killing her.
Lara later pleaded guilty and received life in prison. The case sparked widespread discussion about hiring practices, background checks, and safety concerns involving private security personnel.

item.title: What Happened to Patrice Endres?
item.description:
In 2004, Georgia hairstylist Patrice Endres disappeared from her salon in Forsyth County during the middle of a workday. Customers arrived to find the salon abandoned, with signs suggesting she had vanished suddenly within a very short window of time.
Months later, Patrice’s remains were discovered in a wooded area, but investigators struggled to determine exactly what happened. Over the years, attention and public speculation frequently focused on people close to the case, including her husband, Rob Endres, though no one has ever been charged in connection with her death.
The case gained renewed attention after being featured in documentaries and true crime series, with many viewers questioning inconsistencies, possible motives, and unanswered details surrounding Patrice’s disappearance and murder. To this day, the case remains unsolved.

media:content.media:title: What Happened to Patrice Endres?
media:content.media:description:
In 2004, Georgia hairstylist Patrice Endres disappeared from her salon in Forsyth County during the middle of a workday. Customers arrived to find the salon abandoned, with signs suggesting she had vanished suddenly within a very short window of time.
Months later, Patrice’s remains were discovered in a wooded area, but investigators struggled to determine exactly what happened. Over the years, attention and public speculation frequently focused on people close to the case, including her husband, Rob Endres, though no one has ever been charged in connection with her death.
The case gained renewed attention after being featured in documentaries and true crime series, with many viewers questioning inconsistencies, possible motives, and unanswered details surrounding Patrice’s disappearance and murder. To this day, the case remains unsolved.
